ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general french fries entry (FOODON_00002694). Voodoo Fries are seasoned fries with cheese sauce, ranch dressing, and cajun seasoning; no exact FoodOn entry exists for this composite product. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Potato products and dishes - Fried potatoes (chips/fries)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers fried potato products. The cheese sauce and ranch toppings are not captured by this code; closest defensible anchor used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept found for seasoned cheese-topped fries as a composite fast-food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large order of Voodoo Fries as served at Wingstop Singapore, approximately 340 g, comprising seasoned fries with cheese sauce, ranch dressing, and cajun seasoning.

nutrient_source.title: French Fries, fast food — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21137); supplemented with USDA Branded Food data for cheese sauce and ranch dressing toppings
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Wingstop Voodoo Fries. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for fast-food seasoned french fries (NDB 21137, ~289 kcal/100g) with proportional contributions from processed cheese sauce (USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170904) and ranch dressing (USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 172949). The composite estimate reflects approximately 75% seasoned fries, 15% cheese sauce, and 10% ranch dressing by weight. Values are rounded to one decimal place. This is a medium-confidence estimate; exact Wingstop nutritional data is not available from an approved primary source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — French fries / fried potato products
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Wingstop Voodoo Fries. GI of 75 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for french fries (deep-fried potato strips), which is consistent with values reported in Atkinson et al. (2021) for fried potato products (GI range 63–87, reference food glucose=100). The cheese sauce and ranch dressing toppings contribute negligible additional glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 36.5 × 3.4) / 100 = 93.1 g GL per 340 g large serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg/100g; ~1632 mg per large serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Voodoo Fries (Large), as a single large serving provides approximately 1632 mg sodium — over 70% of the WHO-recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — primarily from the seasoning blend, processed cheese sauce, and ranch dressing.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 75) and very high glycemic load per serving (~93 GL units)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Voodoo Fries (Large) due to the very high glycemic load (~93 GL per large serving) driven by deep-fried starchy potato and the high GI of 75, which will cause a rapid and substantial postprandial blood glucose rise; if consumed, portion size must be drastically reduced and paired with low-GI foods and adequate protein.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium from deep-frying and processed sauce toppings
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Voodoo Fries (Large), as the combination of high saturated fat (~14 g per serving), trans fat (~0.3 g per serving), and very high sodium (~1632 mg per serving) from the deep-frying process and processed cheese and ranch toppings collectively elevates LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Voodoo Fries (Large) is a signature side dish offered at Wingstop restaurants in Singapore, including outlets at City Square Mall and Funan. The dish consists of a large portion of deep-fried, seasoned potato fries — cut in the classic fast-food strip style — coated with a proprietary cajun spice blend that imparts a mildly spicy, smoky, and savoury flavour. The fries are generously topped with a warm processed cheese sauce that adds a creamy, tangy richness, and finished with a drizzle of ranch dressing for a cool, herbaceous contrast. The result is a layered flavour profile combining heat from the cajun seasoning, umami from the cheese, and the cooling creaminess of ranch. Texturally, the fries are crisp on the exterior and soft inside, with the sauces adding a moist, indulgent coating. The dish is classified as ultra-processed and is high in energy (~298 kcal/100g), total fat (~15.8 g/100g), saturated fat (~4.1 g/100g), and sodium (~480 mg/100g). The glycemic index is high (GI 75), and the glycemic load per large serving is very high (~93 GL units), making this dish clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is halal-certified at Wingstop Singapore and is vegetarian but not vegan due to dairy-containing sauces. It is sold at a fixed price of SGD 6.75 and is listed under the Snacks food group. Nutritionally, it provides moderate dietary fibre (~2.8 g/100g) and some potassium (~420 mg/100g) from the potato base, but these benefits are outweighed by the high sodium, fat, and glycemic burden for individuals with diet-sensitive conditions.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for Wingstop Voodoo Fries (Large) is available from an approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded entry, or equivalent). Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for fast-food seasoned french fries with proportional contributions from USDA FoodData Central entries for processed cheese sauce and ranch dressing. GI is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database for fried potato products as an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21137 for fast-food french fries) and USDA FoodData Central branded entries for processed cheese sauce (FDC ID 170904) and ranch dressing (FDC ID 172949). No exact Wingstop-specific nutritional panel was available from an approved source. 2. Serving size of 340 g is an estimate for a large fast-food fries portion; Wingstop Singapore does not publish exact gram weights in an approved nutritional database. 3. Vitamin D is reported as -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. Selenium is reported as -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data for this product. 4. Trans fat is estimated at 0.1 g/100g based on USDA data for commercially deep-fried potato products; this may vary by oil type used at Wingstop Singapore. 5. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for deep-fried potato strips; no direct GI measurement for Voodoo Fries exists in the approved literature. 6. The dish is listed as halal on field survey data based on Wingstop Singapore's halal certification; this is preserved from the input record. 7. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the dish is served in Singapore. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA composite data and should be treated as approximate.
